conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances. Let me start by reading the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **How many children are you inquiring about for Child Benefit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm interested in finding out if I can claim Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 8 years old and lives with me here in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old and lives with you in Manchester.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you a few more questions:

**Are you currently living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester, as I mentioned.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

Next question: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's good to know, thank you.

One more question: **Is Alex currently in hospital, in care, or in any residential accommodation, or does he live at home with you full-time?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, actually, I'm fostering Alex. He lives at home with me full-time though.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that important information. 

Since you're fostering Alex, I need to ask: **Is the local council paying anything towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't get anything from the council for him. I'm covering everything myself.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me:
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
- He lives with you full-time in the UK (Manchester)
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- You are fostering Alex
- The local council is not paying anything towards his accommodation or maintenance
- He is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation

According to the official guidance, you are eligible to get Child Benefit if you foster a child, as long as the local council is not paying anything towards their accommodation or maintenance. Since the council is not paying anything and you're covering everything yourself, you meet this requirement.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Since you're fostering Alex and the local council is not paying anything towards his accommodation or maintenance, you meet all the requirements for Child Benefit. Alex is under 16, lives with you in the UK, and nobody else is claiming for him.

You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit for Alex. If you need help with the application process, you can find information on the GOV.UK website or contact the Child Benefit Office directly.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16) and lives with the claimant in the UK. The claimant is fostering Alex, and according to the eligibility rules, you can get Child Benefit if you foster a child as long as the local council is not paying anything towards their accommodation or maintenance. The claimant confirmed that the local council is not paying anything and they are covering everything themselves. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is fostering an 8-year-old child (Alex) who lives with them full-time in Manchester, UK. The local council is not contributing towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. Under UK Child Benefit rules, foster carers are eligible for Child Benefit as long as the local council is not paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.

Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

The local council is not contributing towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. Under UK Child Benefit rules, foster carers are eligible for Child Benefit as long as the local council is not paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.
